title: senecio integrifolius subsp. capitatus
intro: Senecio integrifolius subsp. capitatus, also known as Jersey knapweed, is a plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Channel Islands and the southwestern coast of England. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating digestive issues, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, Jersey knapweed is often used as a tea or tincture, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions with other medications.
